---
title: "Explainable, Trustworthy & Human-Centered AI"
subtitle: "Responsible, fair, interpretable AI for scientific and societal decision-making"
description: "BioMIND research on explainable AI, responsible AI, fairness, human-centered AI, trustworthy BioAI, and human-machine teams."
---

## Overview

BioMIND develops **responsible and interpretable AI systems** that support scientific discovery and human decision-making. This area includes **explainable AI**, **fairness**, **human-centered AI**, **responsible AI**, **cognitive compatibility**, **bias mitigation**, and **trustworthy AI for biological and social systems**.

## Key themes

::: {.bm-grid .bm-grid-2}

::: {.bm-card}
### Explainable AI
Mechanistic interpretability, attribution, concept-based explanations, and faithfulness.
:::

::: {.bm-card}
### Responsible AI
Ethics, governance, and societal-impact analysis for AI in scientific and societal contexts.
:::

::: {.bm-card}
### Fairness and bias mitigation
Audit methodologies, fairness metrics, and bias-aware learning across high-stakes domains.
:::

::: {.bm-card}
### Human-centered AI
AI systems designed around human needs, abilities, and values.
:::

::: {.bm-card}
### Human-machine teams
Mixed-initiative interfaces, shared authority, and collaborative AI for expert workflows.
:::

::: {.bm-card}
### Trustworthy BioAI
Calibrated, interpretable, and human-aligned AI for biological discovery.
:::

::: {.bm-card}
### Interpretable molecular ML
Explanations and probes for molecular generative and predictive models.
:::

::: {.bm-card}
### Social cybersecurity
The interplay of AI, networks, and adversarial information dynamics.
:::

:::

## Flagship contribution

::: {.bm-callout}
**[Six Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence Grand Challenges](https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/10447318.2022.2153320).** Ozmen Garibay *et al.*, *International Journal of Human-Computer Interaction*, 2023.

A major international collaboration identifying grand challenges for creating AI technologies that are ethical, fair, human-centered, and beneficial to society.
:::

## Related faculty

- [Ozlem Ozmen Garibay](../people/faculty.qmd#ozlem-ozmen-garibay) — Human-CAIR
- [Ivan I. Garibay](../people/faculty.qmd#ivan-i-garibay) — CASL (AI explainability and information dynamics)
- [Niloofar Yousefi](../people/faculty.qmd#niloofar-yousefi) — AI-AIR

## Representative publications

See the [Publications](../publications/index.qmd) page, under *Selected Publications* and *Human-Centered and Trustworthy AI*.
